title: Flavin-containing amine oxidoreductase

infobox.value: are a family of various [amine oxidases](/wiki/Amine_oxidase "Amine oxidase"), including maize [polyamine](/wiki/Polyamine "Polyamine") oxidase (PAO),[[2]](#cite_note-PUB00001716-2) L-[amino acid](/wiki/Amino_acid "Amino acid") oxidases (LAO) and various [flavin](/wiki/Flavin_group "Flavin group") containing [monoamine oxidases](/wiki/Monoamine_oxidase "Monoamine oxidase") (MAO). The aligned region includes the flavin binding site of these enzymes. In vertebrates, MAO plays an important role in regulating the intracellular levels of amines via their oxidation; these include various [neurotransmitters](/wiki/Neurotransmitter "Neurotransmitter"), [neurotoxins](/wiki/Neurotoxin "Neurotoxin") and [trace amines](/wiki/Trace_amine "Trace amine").[[3]](#cite_note-PUB00002991-3) In lower eukaryotes such as aspergillus and in bacteria the main role of amine oxidases is to provide a source of ammonium.[[4]](#cite_note-PUB00003770-4) PAOs in plants, bacteria and protozoa oxidise [spermidine](/wiki/Spermidine "Spermidine") and [spermine](/wiki/Spermine "Spermine") to an [aminobutyral](/w/index.php?title=Aminobutyral&action=edit&redlink=1 "Aminobutyral (page does not exist)"), [diaminopropane](/wiki/1,3-Diaminopropane "1,3-Diaminopropane") and [hydrogen peroxide](/wiki/Hydrogen_peroxide "Hydrogen peroxide") and are involved in the [catabolism](/wiki/Catabolism "Catabolism") of [polyamines](/wiki/Polyamine "Polyamine").[[2]](#cite_note-PUB00001716-2) Other members of this family include [tryptophan 2-monooxygenase](/wiki/Tryptophan_2-monooxygenase "Tryptophan 2-monooxygenase"), [putrescine oxidase](/wiki/Putrescine_oxidase "Putrescine oxidase"), [corticosteroid](/wiki/Corticosteroid "Corticosteroid")-binding proteins, and antibacterial glycoproteins.

Binda C, Coda A, Angelini R, Federico R, Ascenzi P, Mattevi A (March 1999). ["A 30-angstrom-long U-shaped catalytic tunnel in the crystal structure of polyamine oxidase"](https://doi.org/10.1016%2Fs0969-2126%2899%2980037-9). *Structure*. **7** (3): 265–76. [doi](/wiki/Doi_(identifier) "Doi (identifier)"):[10.1016/s0969-2126(99)80037-9](https://doi.org/10.1016%2Fs0969-2126%2899%2980037-9). [PMID](/wiki/PMID_(identifier) "PMID (identifier)") [10368296](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/10368296).

Tsugeno Y, Ito A (1997). ["A key amino acid responsible for substrate selectivity of monoamine oxidase A and B"](https://doi.org/10.1074%2Fjbc.272.22.14033). *J. Biol. Chem*. **272** (22): 14033–14036. [doi](/wiki/Doi_(identifier) "Doi (identifier)"):[10.1074/jbc.272.22.14033](https://doi.org/10.1074%2Fjbc.272.22.14033). [PMID](/wiki/PMID_(identifier) "PMID (identifier)") [9162023](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/9162023).

Lerch K, Schilling B (1995). "Cloning, sequencing and heterologous expression of the monoamine oxidase gene from Aspergillus niger". *Mol. Gen. Genet*. **247** (4): 430–438. [doi](/wiki/Doi_(identifier) "Doi (identifier)"):[10.1007/BF00293144](https://doi.org/10.1007%2FBF00293144). [PMID](/wiki/PMID_(identifier) "PMID (identifier)") [7770050](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/7770050). [S2CID](/wiki/S2CID_(identifier) "S2CID (identifier)") [26600138](https://api.semanticscholar.org/CorpusID:26600138).

The aligned region includes the flavin binding site of these enzymes. In vertebrates, MAO plays an important role in regulating the intracellular levels of amines via their oxidation; these include various [neurotransmitters](/wiki/Neurotransmitter "Neurotransmitter"), [neurotoxins](/wiki/Neurotoxin "Neurotoxin") and [trace amines](/wiki/Trace_amine "Trace amine").[[3]](#cite_note-PUB00002991-3) In lower eukaryotes such as aspergillus and in bacteria the main role of amine oxidases is to provide a source of ammonium.[[4]](#cite_note-PUB00003770-4) PAOs in plants, bacteria and protozoa oxidise [spermidine](/wiki/Spermidine "Spermidine") and [spermine](/wiki/Spermine "Spermine") to an [aminobutyral](/w/index.php?title=Aminobutyral&action=edit&redlink=1 "Aminobutyral (page does not exist)"), [diaminopropane](/wiki/1,3-Diaminopropane "1,3-Diaminopropane") and [hydrogen peroxide](/wiki/Hydrogen_peroxide "Hydrogen peroxide") and are involved in the [catabolism](/wiki/Catabolism "Catabolism") of [polyamines](/wiki/Polyamine "Polyamine").[[2]](#cite_note-PUB00001716-2) Other members of this family include [tryptophan 2-monooxygenase](/wiki/Tryptophan_2-monooxygenase "Tryptophan 2-monooxygenase"), [putrescine oxidase](/wiki/Putrescine_oxidase "Putrescine oxidase"), [corticosteroid](/wiki/Corticosteroid "Corticosteroid")-binding proteins, and antibacterial glycoproteins.

**Flavin-containing amine oxidoreductases** are a family of various [amine oxidases](/wiki/Amine_oxidase "Amine oxidase"), including maize [polyamine](/wiki/Polyamine "Polyamine") oxidase (PAO),[[2]](#cite_note-PUB00001716-2) L-[amino acid](/wiki/Amino_acid "Amino acid") oxidases (LAO) and various [flavin](/wiki/Flavin_group "Flavin group") containing [monoamine oxidases](/wiki/Monoamine_oxidase "Monoamine oxidase") (MAO). The aligned region includes the flavin binding site of these enzymes. In vertebrates, MAO plays an important role in regulating the intracellular levels of amines via their oxidation; these include various [neurotransmitters](/wiki/Neurotransmitter "Neurotransmitter"), [neurotoxins](/wiki/Neurotoxin "Neurotoxin") and [trace amines](/wiki/Trace_amine "Trace amine").[[3]](#cite_note-PUB00002991-3) In lower eukaryotes such as aspergillus and in bacteria the main role of amine oxidases is to provide a source of ammonium.[[4]](#cite_note-PUB00003770-4) PAOs in plants, bacteria and protozoa oxidise [spermidine](/wiki/Spermidine "Spermidine") and [spermine](/wiki/Spermine "Spermine") to an [aminobutyral](/w/index.php?title=Aminobutyral&action=edit&redlink=1 "Aminobutyral (page does not exist)"), [diaminopropane](/wiki/1,3-Diaminopropane "1,3-Diaminopropane") and [hydrogen peroxide](/wiki/Hydrogen_peroxide "Hydrogen peroxide") and are involved in the [catabolism](/wiki/Catabolism "Catabolism") of [polyamines](/wiki/Polyamine "Polyamine").[[2]](#cite_note-PUB00001716-2) Other members of this family include [tryptophan 2-monooxygenase](/wiki/Tryptophan_2-monooxygenase "Tryptophan 2-monooxygenase"), [putrescine oxidase](/wiki/Putrescine_oxidase "Putrescine oxidase"), [corticosteroid](/wiki/Corticosteroid "Corticosteroid")-binding proteins, and antibacterial glycoproteins.

## Human proteins containing this domain

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Flavin-containing_amine_oxidoreductase&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Human proteins containing this domain")]

[AOF1](/wiki/AOF1 "AOF1"); [AOF2](/wiki/AOF2 "AOF2"); [IL4I1](/w/index.php?title=IL4I1&action=edit&redlink=1 "IL4I1 (page does not exist)"); [MAOA](/wiki/MAOA "MAOA"); [MAOB](/wiki/MAOB "MAOB"); [PAOX](/wiki/PAOX "PAOX"); [PPOX](/wiki/PPOX "PPOX"); [SMOX](/wiki/SMOX "SMOX");
